title: Change FSNotes++ bundle ID from co.fluder.FSNotes to app.fsnotes.fork-gjouret
description: Sharing co.fluder.FSNotes with the upstream /Applications/FSNotes.app causes LaunchServices ambiguity: open_application, osascript quit, and lsappinfo can all target either binary. This has caused multiple wrong-app live verifications (blockquote fragment 2026-04-19, fsnotes-ibj attachment redraw 2026-04-29, fsnotes-hhm dark-mode glyph 2026-04-29). Changing the bundle ID to app.fsnotes.fork-gjouret eliminates the entire class. Steps: (1) update PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER in project.pbxproj for the FSNotes app target. CFBundleName stays 'FSNotes++'. (2) Add a one-shot migration helper that runs on first launch under the new bundle ID — read ~/Library/Preferences/co.fluder.FSNotes.plist and copy keys into the new domain. (3) Notes folder: debug builds are unsandboxed (CLAUDE.md), so direct path access to ~/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~co~fluder~fsnotes/Documents/ continues to work without entitlement changes for dev. Release-build entitlement migration is out-of-scope for this bead. (4) Update CLAUDE.md and the feedback_verify_correct_app_binary.md memory: the lsappinfo + bundlepath check should now expect the new bundle ID. (5) Verify by quitting all FSNotes, launching FSNotes++.app via explicit path, confirming lsappinfo info -only bundlepath app.fsnotes.fork-gjouret returns ~/Applications/FSNotes++.app and lsappinfo for co.fluder.FSNotes returns nothing or the upstream path.
acceptance_criteria: lsappinfo info -only bundlepath app.fsnotes.fork-gjouret returns ~/Applications/FSNotes++.app; FSNotes++ launches via Bash 'open ~/Applications/FSNotes++.app'; existing notes (in ~/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~co~fluder~fsnotes/Documents/) still load; pin state survives migration; rule7-gate clean; full pure test suite passes.

title:
Last table cell (bottom-right): typing inserts
per character — REGRESSION
description:
Typing in the bottom-right cell of a table inserts a literal
for every character typed. This is a regression — was fixed previously and has reappeared. Check whether the recent newline-substitution fix (substitute newline with U+2028 in cell content) regressed last-cell newline handling, or whether last-cell terminator logic is treating EOL specially.
notes:
Three live-integration tests added to BugFsnotes95mTests.swift, all PASS at HEAD (8656912): 1. test_typeInLastCell_viaHandleTableCellEdit_doesNotInsertBr — empty last cell, type 'X', expect [.text("X")]. 2. test_typeAfterExistingTextInLastCell_doesNotInsertBr — populated last cell ('bb'), type 'X' at end, expect [.text("bbX")]. 3. test_multipleConsecutiveKeystrokesInLastCell_dontAccumulateBr — empty last cell, type 'X','Y','Z' in succession, expect monotonic [.text("X")] -> [.text("XY")] -> [.text("XYZ")] with no .rawHTML(
) between. All three exercise editor.handleEditViaBlockModel via EditorHarness.type() — the same primitive insertText() routes through under the live app. The bug as described in the bead does NOT reproduce in any of these scaffolds. Pure-fn primitive (replaceTableCellInline) was already known to pass. Possible explanations: (a) cascade closure — the bug was already fixed by another agent's commit (most likely candidate: f5596e4 / 5a1c65c which touched the input pipeline); (b) the regression requires real keyboard events / window-focus behavior the offscreen EditorHarness doesn't replicate; (c) the regression specifically requires multi-line-content cells using
-substituted U+2028 already in the cell. Live-app verification attempted but blocked by Claude.app focus issues. Recommend the bead be left for user manual verification: open a note containing a 2x2 table, click into bottom-right cell, type characters, observe whether 'X
Y
Z' appears or just 'XYZ'. If clean, close as cascade with the SHA of the test-green run; if reproduces, the next investigator has 3 well-formed XCTExpectFailure-ready scaffolds to wrap and bisect-anchor.

title: Table cell: typed text appears LEFT of cursor (1-2 char offset)
description: When typing in a table cell, characters appear to the left of the visible cursor by 1-2 character widths. The caret and the inserted glyphs disagree on position. Likely a coord-space mismatch in TableLayoutFragment caret rect computation (per feedback_coord_space_offset_bugs.md). Compare cell fragment-local vs. container vs. view origins.
design: no, now it’s worse: the cursor still appears 1-2 characters to the right of new characters typed and now also 1 line above the current line
notes: DONE: 2 integration regression tests added (click+type in data/header cells). Verify caret rect matches expected position after typing in table cell. Commit: 66e6284

title: META: bullet/Todo glyphs disappear during edit, reappear on scroll
description: Persistent throughout the Refactor 4 effort. While editing in a note, bullet and Todo (checked and unchecked) glyphs vanish; scrolling forces a redraw and they come back. This is a meta-bug — likely the redraw signal is not propagating from the edit applier to the attachment view providers, or fragment invalidation is failing to trigger an attachment re-display.
notes: QA 2026-04-29 (v3 / commit 63d3873): user reports flicker STILL occurring after dropping the attachment.image tickle and keeping only layoutViewport(). Tickle was the suspected flicker cause in v1, but removing it didn't help — flicker has a different source. Reopened to investigate observed behaviour before next attempt.

title: Test suite hangs on HeaderTests.test_headerFonts_areBold
description: The full xcodebuild test run wedges on 'FSNotesTests.HeaderTests.test_headerFonts_areBold' with no completion event. Observed during a clean run on 2026-04-29: 2657 cases scheduled, 1293 passed before the hang, 9 unrelated failures earlier in the run. The test entered 'started' state at ~10:17 and never produced 'passed' or 'failed' over the next 10+ minutes; xcodebuild process remains alive (state SN, sleeping) but writes no further output to either /tmp/xctest.log or the xcresult bundle. This blocks the suite from completing — every full run must be killed manually before any 'BUILD SUCCEEDED'/'TEST SUCCEEDED' conclusion is reached. Repro: xcodebuild test -workspace FSNotes.xcworkspace -scheme FSNotes -destination 'platform=macOS' > /tmp/xctest.log 2>&1. Wait ~1 minute past last 'Test Case' entry; if HeaderTests is the last started case with no terminal status, the hang has reproduced. Likely cause: the test depends on a window/font load that doesn't complete in the test environment — header font assertions probably need a live AppKit context that the bare xctest runner doesn't fully provide. Need to either gate this test on a window-context precondition, port the assertion to a pure-function test on Theme/DocumentRenderer, or skip it under non-interactive runs.
notes: Regression scaffold: Tests/Regression/BugFsnotes5fbTests.swift. Runs the same SourceRenderer pipeline with a 10s watchdog. ISOLATION PASSES at master (HEAD=18e939b) in 0.115s. Original HeaderTests.test_headerFonts_areBold ALSO passes in isolation in 0.112s. HOWEVER, the bead description specifies the hang is observed in a FULL test run after ~1293 prior cases — the scaffold does NOT reproduce the cross-test state leakage. Next step: run full xcodebuild test and observe whether the suite-context hang reproduces at HEAD. If yes, investigate prior tests for state leakage. If no, close bead with full-suite SHA evidence.

title: Code block / Mermaid edit toggle button not appearing on hover
description: Hovering inside a code block should show a </> button in the top-right corner that toggles edit mode. Same for Mermaid blocks. Neither the button nor the edit mode toggle is working — the overlay is not visible. Check CodeBlockEditToggleOverlay for why it's not mounting or not becoming visible on hover.

title: Return in L1 list item followed by L2 creates new L1 at bottom of list, not after current L1
description: When pressing Return in an L1 list item that has L2 (or deeper) items between it and the next L1, the new blank L1 is inserted at the bottom of the entire list instead of immediately after the current L1. Example: L1/A, L2/a, L2/b, L1/B — pressing Return at end of L1/A should insert a new L1 between L2/b and L1/B, but it inserts it after L1/B (bottom of list).
notes: Unclaimed by DEEPSEEK — assigned to claude-A

title: Migrate Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt to bd as single source of truth
description: DiscoveredBugs.txt currently lists combinatorial-runner failure scenarios; the runner XCTExpectFailure-wraps each entry. Pre-dates the bd workflow. Migrate every line to a bd issue with --label=combinatorial; encode the scenario label in --notes. Update CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift to query 'bd list --label=combinatorial --status=open' at startup and wrap the matching scenarios. Then delete DiscoveredBugs.txt. Acceptance: bd is the only authoritative bug list; the .txt is removed; the suite stays green; closing a bead un-wraps its XCTExpectFailure on next runner invocation.
design: DiscoveredBugs.txt  should be removed—all bugs tracked in Beadbox—this should already be done-verify

title: Image resize: hand cursor offset below handle; only TR/BL handles work
description: Two related symptoms when clicking an image to resize: (1) the hand cursor appears below the resize handle instead of on top of it — coord-space offset of the cursor hit zone, sibling to the table-cell-text-left-of-cursor pattern; (2) resizing is only possible from the top-right and bottom-left handles, the other six do not respond. Likely the same root cause — handle hit-test rects are computed with a wrong origin so only the corners that happen to land on the offset rects fire. Check ImageSelectionHandleDrawer.swift and the InlineImageView mouseDown/cursorUpdate path for the same fragment-local-vs-view-coord mismatch flagged in feedback_coord_space_offset_bugs.md.
notes:
DEEPSEEK investigation:
Root cause: InlineImageView.handleCenters and handleHitTest use bounds.minX/maxX/minY/maxY, but the image uses imageScaling = .scaleProportionallyUpOrDown. The visible image rect is smaller than the view bounds, so handles are drawn at view corners — far from the actual image edges. Also, handle names are Y-flipped relative to Cocoa unflipped coordinates: (minX, minY) is bottom-left in Cocoa but mapped to .topLeft in the enum.

Fix: compute the actual displayed image rect using contentsRect or the backing NSImage.size + scale factor, and use THAT rect for handle positions. Or compute the image-to-view scale and inset handle positions accordingly.

title: Mode-aware render cache for MathJax/Mermaid/QuickLook (light <-> dark)
description: MathJax and Mermaid renders are cached as SVGs/images with a white background — fine in Light mode, jarring in Dark mode. Make the render cache mode-aware: when system appearance changes Light <-> Dark, re-render the cached attachments so background is transparent or matches the note background. Same for QuickLook thumbnails (Numbers, Excel, etc.). Listen for NSAppearance change notifications and invalidate the cache.
notes: DONE: appearance-keyed QuickLookThumbnailCache lands in HEAD. Companion fsnotes-237 (commit 3707f0e) already shipped the BlockRenderer half (cache key includes isDark, transparent CSS, drawsBackground=false). This bead closes the QuickLook gap: cache key is now '<dark|light>:<url-absoluteString>' so a Light <-> Dark switch produces a cache miss and re-renders Numbers/Pages/Excel thumbnails under the correct mode. viewDidChangeEffectiveAppearance was already calling refillEditArea(force:true) -> QuickLookAttachmentProcessor reruns -> fresh InlineQuickLookView -> reads new key -> right-mode thumbnail. Mermaid/math fragments rebuild in the same refill, so per-fragment renderedImage caches drop to nil and ensureRenderRequested triggers a fresh BlockRenderer.render() that misses on the new appearance discriminator. Regression tests: Tests/Regression/BugFsnotes64cTests.swift (4 tests: light/dark distinct slots, cross-mode lookup miss, URL+mode composition, BlockRenderer disk cache file appearance-specific). Existing Tests/InlineQuickLookScrollPropagationTests.swift updated to the new API. All 4 new + 17 existing tests pass.
close_reason: test green at 8656912; companion to fsnotes-237 (3707f0e). QuickLookThumbnailCache now appearance-keyed; mermaid/math fragments rebuild on viewDidChangeEffectiveAppearance via existing refillEditArea(force:true) path.

title: Dark mode: Todo checkbox glyph drawn black on dark gray (invisible)
description: The Todo checkbox glyph in dark mode is drawn black against the dark gray editor background — invisible. Both unchecked and checked states should use the foreground / secondaryLabel color (which adapts to mode).
notes: DONE: fix in HEAD — rebuild CheckboxGlyphView.cachedImage in viewDidChangeEffectiveAppearance() instead of caching once at init. Wraps the tinted(with:) call in performAsCurrentDrawingAppearance to force secondaryLabelColor to resolve under the view's actual effective appearance. ListRenderer.swift:828-908. Light-mode unchanged (verified with deployed binary). Dark-mode verification pending: System Settings → Appearance → Dark; open a note with Todo items; checkboxes should now be visible (light gray on dark bg) instead of invisible (dark gray on dark bg).

title: Mathjax/Mermaid: render with transparent background instead of white/black
description: Mathjax and mermaid currently render against a fixed white (or black, in dark mode) background. Render with a transparent background so the diagram blends with the note background regardless of theme.
notes: DONE: fix in HEAD. CSS body background changed to 'transparent' for both .mermaid and .math cases (FSNotes/Helpers/BlockRenderer.swift). WKWebView configured with drawsBackground=false + underPageBackgroundColor=.clear so the snapshot honors the CSS transparency. Cache key now includes light/dark appearance so a mode switch produces a cache miss instead of a stale-colored SVG. Verification needed: open a note containing a mermaid diagram or math block in BOTH light and dark mode, confirm the diagram blends with the editor bg (no white panel, no black panel).

title: Format / Code block: missing language hint causes broken render
description: Selecting text and choosing Format / Code block wraps the selection in fences but does not prompt for or insert a language identifier. Without one, syntax highlighting cannot pick a renderer. Needs a language picker or a 'text' default.

title: ensureLayout called once per attachment swap instead of once at end
description: Mermaid/math source-to-attachment swap: ensureLayout fires 3 times per replacement instead of once after all replacements. Wrap the batch in a single beginEditing/endEditing and call ensureLayout once at the end.

title: Mermaid: code-block insert adds blank line before fences; does not render
description: Format / Code block / Mermaid: inserts a blank line before the fence instead of placing the cursor inside the fences. After typing mermaid syntax, the diagram is not rendered.

title: Attachment scroll: does not pass through to note at top/bottom edge
description: Long attachment (e.g. a log file QuickLook) inside a note: scrolling past the top or bottom of the attachment should pass the wheel event to the note's scroll view (Obsidian behavior). Currently attachment swallows the event.

title: Horizontal line button: cursor not positioned right of new HR
description: Toolbar HR insert: line draws correctly but the caret stays at the previous position rather than landing on the line after the HR. EditingOps.insertHorizontalRule needs to set the cursor to the start of the paragraph after the HR.

title: Move Item Up/Down moves entire list, not selected line
description: Format / Move Item / Move Selected Lines Down or Up moves the whole list block, not the cursor's current list item. Selection logic uses the parent list block instead of the child block at the cursor's path.

title: Insert Link toolbar: URL not clickable until app reload
description: Insert Link button inserts [https://...](https://...) markdown. The link does not render as clickable until the app is restarted (clicking off and back to the note does not help). Inline parse not re-run after toolbar insert.
notes: DONE: 2 regression tests. wrapInLink creates proper .link inlines with .link attributes. Commit: ece8c32

title: Wikilink picker leaves cursor on a new line after selection
description: Type [[, pick a note with Tab/Enter. The caret lands on the next line; subsequent typing visually appears immediately after the link, but the structural newline should not be there. Cursor should land right after the closing ]].
notes: DONE: Added reparseCurrentBlockInlines() call after applyBlockModelResult in insertWikiCompletion. Fix + 2 regression tests. Commit: f4feeb9

title: Paste table under H1: blank line auto-inserted before table
description: Pasting a table immediately under the H1 title adds an unrequested blank line between the heading and the table. Paste path likely synthesises a separator that is not needed.

title: Undo of table deletion does not restore the table
description: Delete a table, press Cmd+Z. Table is not restored. UndoJournal entry for table deletion likely uses a Tier A inverse instead of a Tier B block snapshot.

title: Numbers paste: table markdown inserted but not rendered until blank line added
description: Pasting a table from Numbers inserts valid table markdown into the note but the WYSIWYG renderer does not pick it up until you append a blank line after the table source. Reparse should fire on paste, not require a trailing blank.

title: Multi-line cell: column handle overlaps header; row handle not resized
description: Pressing Return inside a cell to add a second line redraws the column handle over the table header. The row handle stays at its old size despite the row now being taller.

title: WYSIWYG to Markdown toggle: text selection not preserved
description: Selecting text in WYSIWYG and toggling to Markdown loses the selection — some other nearby range is selected instead. Same family as the cursor-position bug; need a projection-to-source range mapper.

title: WYSIWYG to Markdown toggle: cursor position lost
description: Toggling from WYSIWYG to Markdown mode places the caret at a seemingly random position rather than the equivalent source offset. Need a projection-to-source offset map preserved across the toggle.

title: Triple-click paragraph + Delete demotes following list line
description: Triple-click a paragraph above a list, press Delete: the paragraph stays, the list item below is demoted to paragraph. Selection granularity probably includes the trailing inter-block separator.

title: New-note title cursor: caret height wrong (body, not L1)
description: Creating a new note places the caret in the title at body-text height. Caret resizes to L1 only after the first character is typed. Set typing attributes for the empty title to L1 on creation.

title: External-editor edits via File / Open External not picked up
description: File / Open External edits text.md directly. After saving, the note is not re-rendered. TextBundle mtime / cached note is not invalidated. Need a file watcher on text.md that triggers reload.

title: Window shrink hides folder/notelist panes; do not reappear on expand
description: Shrinking the app window narrow enough hides the folder and note-list panes (width clamped to 0). Expanding the window does not restore them. Pane width should re-grow from 0 when window grows.

title: Print: non-image/non-mermaid attachments skip QuickLook preview
description: Printing a note with a non-image, non-mermaid attachment (e.g. .numbers spreadsheet) omits the QuickLook preview shown in WYSIWYG. Print pipeline must render attachment view providers, not the raw source.

title: Phase5aDeleteBypassFixTests.test_insertCodeSpan_emptySelection_doesNotTripAssertion: code span markers not inserted
description:
Tests/Phase5aDeleteBypassFixTests.swift:206 fails:
  'XCTAssertEqual failed: ("Optional(\"hello\")") is not equal to ("Optional(\"hello\u0060\u0060\")")'

The test inserts an inline code span on an empty selection; expected resulting text is hello (two backticks appended), actual is hello (no markers). So the insertCodeSpan-with-empty-selection path is now a no-op that returns the original text rather than a code-span scaffold. Either (a) the empty-selection branch was changed to do nothing intentionally and the test wasn't updated, or (b) a bug in EditingOps.insertCodeSpan / toggleInlineTrait collapsed the empty-selection insertion. The test name implies its primary purpose is to verify the StorageWriteGuard debug assertion isn't tripped (a Phase 5a invariant); the assertion behavior may be correct now, but the markup-insertion side effect regressed.

Inspect EditingOps.insertCodeSpan / EditTextView formatting wiring; either fix the missing markers or update the test if the intentional behavior is no-op-on-empty-selection (and document why).

title: Phase1OllamaProviderTests.testOllamaStreamParser_handlesPartialLines: 'main flush' expectation times out after 1s
description:
Tests/Phase1OllamaProviderTests.swift:184 fails:
  'Asynchronous wait failed: Exceeded timeout of 1 seconds, with unfulfilled expectations: "main flush".'

The Ollama NDJSON stream parser test feeds partial lines and expects them to coalesce and flush on the main queue inside 1s. The flush expectation is never fulfilled. Likely related to the MCP wired-bridge timeout cluster (fsnotes-ptr) — same shape: an XCTestExpectation that depends on a callback marshaling back to the test thread that never arrives. Candidates:
  (a) Stream parser dispatches to a queue the test isn't draining (RunLoop.main vs DispatchQueue.main timing).
  (b) The 'partial line' branch never emits because the parser is gated on a newline that the test never feeds.
  (c) Recent changes to OllamaProvider.swift broke the partial-line accumulator.

Inspect OllamaProvider stream-parsing code path and the test's expectation-fulfillment site at line 184; cross-check against fsnotes-ptr.

title: paragraph + atStart + pressBackspace produces block-count delta -2 instead of -1
description:
Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift:501 fails:
  '[paragraph | atStart | pressBackspace | empty] block-count delta -2 does not match FSM row paragraph | atStart | pressBackspace → mergeWithPrevious expected window -1...-1. before=5 after=3.'

The FSM transition table declares pressing backspace at the start of a paragraph merges with the previous block (delta -1: one block disappears). The live edit instead produces a delta of -2 — two blocks disappear. Likely candidates:
  (a) A blankLine separator between the paragraphs is being consumed in addition to the merged paragraph.
  (b) The merge is taking the predecessor + a trailing blank, eating both.
  (c) EditingOps.deleteAtBoundary or mergeAdjacentBlocks composes two structural changes where the contract claims one.

Fix lives in EditingOps for the pressBackspace-at-start-of-paragraph case — the EditContract.declaredActions must match the actual count delta. Either correct the action sequence (e.g. add an explicit .deleteBlock for the swallowed blankLine) or change the merge primitive to leave the blankLine in place.

title: numberedList + pressTab does not round-trip through serialize→parse
description:
11 combinatorial scenarios in Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift:264 fail the strong invariant HTML(parse(serialize(doc))) == HTML(doc), all with the pattern [numberedList | <cursor> | pressTab | <selection>]:
  • numberedList × {atStart, midContent, atEnd, onEmptyBlock} × {empty, intraBlock, crossBlock}
  • Plus the corresponding pressTab-bearing sequences in test_combinatorialSequences: type.tab, tab.type, tab.tab, type.type.tab, type.tab.type, type.tab.ret, type.tab.bks, type.tab.tab.

Failure message: 'live Document does NOT round-trip through serialize→parse. Live HTML differs from re-parsed HTML — this state cannot survive save+reload.'

Implication: pressing Tab inside a numbered list produces a Document whose serialized markdown re-parses into a different Document. The user cannot save and reload without losing/changing structure. Likely cause: indent/outdent of an ordered-list item creates structure (e.g. nested ordered list with parent renumbered, or list-with-orphan continuation) that MarkdownSerializer emits in a form MarkdownParser then re-reads as a different shape — strong candidate is the marker/indent emission for nested ordered children. Per ARCHITECTURE.md → Outstanding bugs surfaced by the sweep, fixes here also need to remove the corresponding entries from Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt to prevent the now-passing scenarios from flipping to 'unexpectedly passed'.

title: BugInventoryRegressionTests bug6/bug8/bug25 fail: missing rows in FSMTransitions.swift
description:
Three tests in Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift fail with identical message 'Bug #N row is missing from FSMTransitions.swift':
  • Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift:139 → bug6 (returnAtStartOfHeading_createsEmptyParagraphBefore)
  • Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift:168 → bug8 (backspaceMergesParagraphs)
  • Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift:461 → bug25 (tab_onNumberedListL1_demotesToL2)

The harness expects fixture rows tagged with the corresponding bugId in Tests/Fixtures/FSMTransitions.swift; those rows are absent. Per ARCHITECTURE.md → Test alignment, bug rows with bugId != nil are wrapped in XCTExpectFailure(strict: true) so they fail-by-design until the FSM is fixed; the missing-row failure here is upstream of that — the regression-test scaffolding can't even find the row to wrap. Fix is to add three rows to FSMTransitions.swift covering the three transitions, with bugId set to 6, 8, and 25 respectively, then either let the strict-XCTExpectFailure path catch them as expected failures or implement the missing FSM behavior.

title: MCP wired-bridge tests time out with 'test never completed'
description: Five MCP tool tests fail with XCTAssertTrue(false) carrying message 'test never completed' after a ~5-second timeout: AppendToNoteToolTests.testHappyPathRoutesThroughWiredBridge (5.063s), ApplyFormattingToolTests.testHappyPathTogglesBoldThroughWiredBridge (5.788s), EditNoteToolTests.testHappyPathRoutesReplaceBlockThroughWiredBridge (5.071s), ExportPDFToolTests.testHappyPathExportsRealPDFThroughWiredBridge (5.690s), and one more in the same family (need to confirm full list once the suite completes — see related hang issue). All five share the 'testHappyPath*ThroughWiredBridge' naming pattern and the 'test never completed' assertion message at ~5s, indicating a shared XCTestExpectation timeout in the wired-bridge test fixture. Likely cause: AppBridge dispatch from the test thread never marshals back to the expectation's fulfill() — either (a) the bridge is gated on something only true at runtime (compositionSession state? main-thread assertion?), (b) MCPServer.handleToolCalls is dispatching to a queue the test isn't draining, or (c) the wired-bridge test harness was written against a previous AppBridge interface and silently skips the fulfill path. Files to inspect: Tests/MCP/AppendToNoteToolTests.swift:154, Tests/MCP/ApplyFormattingToolTests.swift:126, similar lines in the other three. AppBridgeImpl write path docs in ARCHITECTURE.md note 'refuses with BridgeEditOutcome.failed if compositionSession.isActive == true' — verify the test isn't tripping that gate.
notes: Confirmed 4 affected tests (not 5 as initially feared): AppendToNoteToolTests/testHappyPathRoutesThroughWiredBridge, ApplyFormattingToolTests/testHappyPathTogglesBoldThroughWiredBridge, EditNoteToolTests/testHappyPathRoutesReplaceBlockThroughWiredBridge, ExportPDFToolTests/testHappyPathExportsRealPDFThroughWiredBridge. Found via: grep -l 'testHappyPath.*ThroughWiredBridge' Tests/MCP/*.swift. The 11 other MCP tool test files (CreateNote, DeleteNote, GetCurrent/Folder/Projects/Tags, ListFolders/Notes, MoveNote, ReadNote, SearchNotes) do not use the wired-bridge fixture and are presumably not affected.

title: Bug #46: blockquote Return-then-Backspace mints fresh slot UUID (13 variants)
description:
Backspace after a Return-split inside a blockquote regenerates the projection slot UUID instead of mutating in place. Harness contract failure (~block@2 slot identity not preserved). 13 sequence variants, single root cause. Same shape as Bug #38 (already fixed for lists) but for blockquote split-and-merge.

Failing scenarios (length-2 + length-3 sequence matrix):
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | type.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.type
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.ret
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.tab
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.stab
- seq | blockquote | ret.tab.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.stab.bks
- seq | blockquote | bks.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | tab.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | stab.ret.bks

Layer: combinatorial sequence sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialSequences

Reference fix: Bug #38 was the same root cause for lists — EditingOps.deleteInList collapses an only-item removal to .paragraph(inline: []) keeping the same slot. The fix here likely involves analogous slot-preservation in the blockquote split/merge path.
acceptance_criteria: All 13 sequence scenarios flip to red in test_combinatorialSequences; 13 lines removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; ~block@2 slot identity preserved across the Return-then-Backspace sequence; round-trip parity holds.

title: Bug #44: numbered-list type.ret.tab sequence doesn't round-trip
description:
Real user flow (extend list with sub-item) produces state that won't survive serialize→parse. Length-3 sequence on a numbered list seed.

Failing scenario:
- seq | numberedList | type.ret.tab

Layer: combinatorial sequence sweep (composability matrix)
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialSequences
acceptance_criteria: Scenario flips to red in test_combinatorialSequences; line removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; round-trip parity holds.

title: Bug #43: display-math block edits don't round-trip (9 variants)
description:
Display-math source presence/absence in the serialized form doesn't match the live projection — likely the rendered fragment owns the display-math source while the projection holds an empty inline. 9 variants, single root cause.

Failing scenarios:
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressReturn | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressBackspace | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressForwardDelete | empty
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressForwardDelete | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressReturn | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressBackspace | empty
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressBackspace | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | empty
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | intraBlock

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
acceptance_criteria: All 9 scenarios flip to red; 9 lines removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; round-trip parity holds for all 9.

title: Bug #42: HR backspace at atStart doesn't round-trip
description:
Backspace at the boundary before an HR should remove it (or merge with the predecessor) but produces state that won't survive serialize→parse.

Failing scenario:
- horizontalRule | atStart | pressBackspace | empty

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
acceptance_criteria: Scenario flips to red; line removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; round-trip parity holds.

title: Bug #41: cross-block selection through a table corrupts state
description:
Cross-block selection that includes a table boundary corrupts the table block on Return / Backspace / ForwardDelete. 3 sequence variants, single root cause.

Failing scenarios (combinatorial single-action matrix):
- table | atEnd | pressReturn | crossBlock
- table | atEnd | pressBackspace | crossBlock
- table | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | crossBlock

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
acceptance_criteria: All 3 scenarios flip to red in CombinatorialCoverageTests; the 3 lines are removed from Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt in the same commit; round-trip parity holds for all 3 scenarios.

title: Bug #40: blockquote forward-delete at atEnd doesn't round-trip
description:
Forward-delete at the end of a blockquote line merges with the next block, but produces state that won't survive save+reload. Same shape as Bug #39 but inside a blockquote — likely shares the merge-into-blockquote-trailing-newline root cause.

Failing scenario (combinatorial single-action matrix):
- blockquote | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | empty

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
Tracking: wrapped in XCTExpectFailure(strict: true) via Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt.
acceptance_criteria: Scenario flips to red ('unexpectedly passed') in CombinatorialCoverageTests; the corresponding line is removed from Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt in the same commit; the round-trip invariant HTML(parse(serialize(doc))) == HTML(doc) holds for the scenario.

title: Bug #39: paragraph forward-delete at atEnd doesn't round-trip
description:
Forward-delete at the end of a paragraph merges with the next block, but produces state that won't survive save+reload (HTML(parse(serialize(doc))) != HTML(doc)).

**Failing scenario** (combinatorial single-action matrix):
`paragraph | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | empty`

**Layer**: combinatorial single-action sweep
**Test**: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
**Tracking**: wrapped in XCTExpectFailure(strict: true) via Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt — entry must be deleted when fixed.

**Acceptance**: scenario flips to red ("unexpectedly passed") in CombinatorialCoverageTests, DiscoveredBugs.txt line removed in same commit.

title: Auto right-alignment for numeric/currency table columns
description: When all rows in a table column are numeric or currency, automatically right-align that column. Could be a one-shot detection at parse time or a heuristic on the table block.
design: or if there’s non-numeric short strings (e.g. < 5) characters—then center justify by default
notes: DONE: autoDetectAlignments in TableGeometry.compute. Numeric columns → right, short text → center. 5 regression tests. Commit: 98fc1fe

title: Sort option on table column handle (asc/desc with arrow)
description: Add a 'Sort' option to the column-handle context menu. First click sorts ascending; second click descending. Show an arrow on the column header indicating sort direction.

title: Decide: keep 'Code block live highlighting' option?
description: Settings / Editor has a 'Code block live highlighting' toggle. Should this be removable (always on)? Decide.

title: Decide: keep 'clickable links' option in Settings/Editor?
description: Settings / Editor has a 'clickable links' toggle. Should this be removable (i.e. always on)? Decide and either remove or document.

title: Investigate: Focus in editor when selecting note — already exists?
description: Settings / Editor / Focus in editor when selecting note. Is this already the implemented behavior? If so, the setting is redundant and can be removed.

title: Investigate: Auto Rename by Title vs First line as title overlap
description: Settings / General / Files Naming / Auto Rename by Title and Settings / Layout / First line as title appear to do the same thing. Confirm — one should be removed or merged.

title: Investigate: what does 'Note auto-selection when body text matched' do?
description: Settings preference 'Note auto selection when body text is matched' — purpose unclear. Investigate behavior, decide whether to keep, rename, or remove.

title: Double-click attachment should open in native macOS app
description: Double-clicking a PDF or other attachment widget should perform the system Open action (open in native app on macOS). Currently no double-click handler — only single-click triggers preview / QuickLook.

title: Multi-cell selection / Clear command for table column or row
description: No way to select a range of cells (e.g. click-drag) to clear their contents at once. Add either click-drag selection (Obsidian-style) or a Clear menu item on the row/column handle context menu.
